_comment: Per-case expected TRANSITIVE effect set. Each case is a function present in ALL FOUR engine fixtures (rust/src/lib.rs, java/Cases.java, candor-ts/Cases.ts, candor-swift/conformance/Cases.swift) with equivalent semantics; the runner asserts every engine infers exactly this set. The cross-engine differential now covers 8 effects: Fs/Net/Exec/Env/Clock (std-only in all four) plus Rand, Db, and Log. Each uses the language's idiomatic API: the bytecode engine (java) needs JDK-expressible forms (SecureRandom, java.sql, java.util.logging), while the SYNTACTIC engines (rust scan, ts AST, swift SwiftSyntax) classify crate/module paths without resolving them (rand, rusqlite, log!/tracing! macros; node:crypto, node:sqlite, winston; SQLite3, NSLog). Closing Log (2026-06-17) took two real engine fixes: candor-scan now classifies log/tracing MACRO paths (it was blind to all logging macros pre-expansion), and candor-ts classifies a κ-modeled package reached via an uninstalled namespace import. Two effects remain per-engine (each impl's own tests), for STRUCTURAL reasons: Clipboard — candor-ts has no node clipboard model (browser-only API); Ipc — no JDK std IPC primitive (sockets are Net).
